_doc: Agent Triforge — Antigravity permission guardrails. Migrated 2026-07-17 from gemini-agents/policies.toml (Gemini CLI policy engine, retired with the Gemini CLI lane).

This file is documentation + a mergeable block: copy the `permissions` object below into workspace settings (templates/.antigravity/settings.json ships it ready for project bootstrap) or merge it into user-level agy settings.
Mapping from each retired policies.toml rule to its Antigravity expression (deny list of action(target) strings):
[[rule]] toolName=run_shell_command commandPrefix='rm -rf'   deny priority=999 ('Destructive rm -rf blocked by policy.')                          -> deny: run_shell_command(rm -rf)
[[rule]] toolName=run_shell_command commandPrefix='git push' deny priority=999 ('git push blocked for subagents — Claude (lead) handles remotes.') -> deny: run_shell_command(git push)
[[rule]] toolName=run_shell_command commandPrefix='sudo'     deny priority=999 ('sudo blocked — subagents run unprivileged.')                      -> deny: run_shell_command(sudo)

[[rule]] subagent=architecture-reviewer toolName=run_shell_command deny priority=200 -> run_shell_command OMITTED from tools in agents/architecture-reviewer.md (the flat deny vocabulary has no per-agent scoping; the allowlist omission IS the denial)
[[rule]] subagent=documentation-writer  toolName=run_shell_command deny priority=200 -> run_shell_command OMITTED from tools in agents/documentation-writer.md (same mechanism)

Enforcement note (probed 2026-07-17 on agy 1.1.3, ops/research/2026-07-probe-record.md): project-tier hooks do NOT fire under `agy -p` (AGY-08), and deny rules do NOT survive --dangerously-skip-permissions (AGY-09) — the invocation helper never passes that flag. Permission-system enforcement at the project tier is therefore unproven, and the per-agent `tools` allowlist is the PRIMARY guardrail; the deny list below is defense-in-depth for the shell-capable agents (codebase-analyst, targeted-researcher).
